definition:
1. earth thrown up, a bank, mound , thrown up against the walls of cities to take them, (Herdotus Historicus):—; a dike  to hinder a river from overflowing, (Herdotus Historicus):—; a dam , (Herdotus Historicus):—; a mole  or pier , carried out into the sea, Lat. moles, (Herdotus Historicus) 
2. like Lat. tumulus, a sepulchral mound, [Refs 5th c.BC, variant datesTragica Adespota 
 
 (ML)
